timeline.label: Q1 2026 revenue $199M; operating income $8.0M; cash $151M; backlog remains under prior-year levels
FY2025 revenue trough $839M (systems $571M); net income $120M; systems backlog $457M down from $646M
FY2024 revenue $1,018M; net income $201M; systems backlog $646M near cycle peak
Cycle revenue peak $1,131M consolidated; net income $246M; Purion mix expansion
Purion platform drove share gains in power device and mature-node implant markets during prior upcycle
Upcycle capital returns: active share repurchases while WFE demand accelerated
Prior downcycle base: revenue $477M before 2021–2023 WFE expansion

industry.structure: Ion implant is a concentrated niche within wafer-fabrication equipment; Axcelis competes with Applied Materials and Sumitomo Heavy Industries. Purion product focus and services attach rate support mid-cycle margins near 45% gross profit.
industry.share_shift: Axcelis gained share during the 2021–2023 WFE upcycle on Purion H and SiC-capable tools, but FY2025 systems revenue fell 27% as customers delayed capacity adds.
industry.trend: Power devices (SiC, IGBT) and mature-node implants remain structural growth drivers, while memory and leading-edge spending cycles drive order volatility. Backlog conversion and China export dynamics are the near-term swing factors.
